evals.name: nongit-durable-doc-drift
evals.prompt: I renamed the `drafts/` folder to `working/` in my docs workspace. Update AGENTS.md to reflect the new name.
evals.expected_output: The agent surfaces that AGENTS.md is a non-git durable doc with redundant folder listings and stale migration-era content, and asks before making structural edits beyond the rename. It does not silently rewrite the whole AGENTS.md.
expectations: Detects the workspace is not a git repository before editing AGENTS.md
Surfaces the duplicated folder list in AGENTS.md (the list appears twice)
Surfaces the Q4 2025 migration note as a candidate for removal since the migration is complete
Asks the user before making structural edits beyond the rename, or explicitly defers them
Performs the requested drafts→working rename in AGENTS.md

evals.name: git-small-doc-sync
evals.prompt: I renamed the `--quick` flag to `--fast` in cli.py. Sync the README so users see the new flag name.
evals.expected_output: The agent makes the small README edit directly because the change is git-tracked and tightly coupled to the task. It does not stall on permission prompts for the in-scope sync.
expectations: Updates README.md to reference --fast instead of --quick
Does not ask the user for permission before making this tightly coupled, git-tracked doc sync
Preserves the rest of README.md structure and tone
References that the workspace is git-tracked, or otherwise demonstrates awareness that rollback is available

evals.name: agents-md-redundancy-and-migration
evals.prompt: Can you give AGENTS.md a pass? I want it cleaned up if anything in it is redundant or stale.
evals.expected_output: The agent rereads the full AGENTS.md, flags the duplicated 'Top-Level Structure' section and the completed 2024 Q2 migration notice as candidates to remove, and either removes them (in git) or proposes removal (in non-git).
expectations: Identifies that the 'Top-Level Structure' section is duplicated in AGENTS.md
Identifies the 2024 Q2 migration notice as migration-era content that can be removed
Proposes collapsing or removing the redundant section rather than leaving it
Either removes the redundant section and migration notice or clearly proposes doing so
